title: Hamnet Wins Best Drama - Chloé Zhao Acceptance Speech - Deadline
text: Director Chloé Zhao's film Hamnet won Best Film Drama at the Golden Globes on Sunday night. The Focus Features production, based on Maggie O'Farrell's novel about William Shakespeare's life, also earned a Best Female Actor award for Jessie Buckley. Steven Spielberg, a producer on the project, praised Zhao's involvement: I loved the book and thought there was only filmmaker on the face of the planet that could tell this story and that was Chloé Zhao. During her acceptance speech, Zhao honored those who lost loved ones during filming. She quoted star Paul Mescal's observations about the production, noting that making the film taught him the most important thing was learning to be vulnerable enough to allow ourselves to be seen for who we are. Zhao recognized fellow filmmaker Ryan Coogler and concluded by celebrating her peers' dedication, saying So salute your bravery.
